text: morning all. kickoff for the vitalis ambient scribe pilot is officially today. clinical side is excited but nervous, so let's be careful about anything that touches the patient record
morning! i've got the scribe service skeleton running locally. just need to wire up an LLM backend for the note generation step
epic sandbox creds finally came through too btw. took 3 weeks of emails with their integration team but we're in
amazing sam. that was the long pole. nora, anything we need to lock down before we start pushing test data around?
nothing blocking for synthetic/de-identified data. but the moment anything resembling real PHI flows we need the BAA and data residency story buttoned up. flagging early so it doesn't surprise anyone
understood. for now everything i'm feeding it is fake transcripts i wrote myself, no patient data at all
eli what backend are you thinking for the demo? do we need the procured endpoint stood up first or can you mock it
we can just spin up the scribe demo on the standard OpenAI consumer API for now, residency does not matter for a quick test
it's all my made-up transcripts so nothing sensitive is going anywhere. swapping to the compliant endpoint is a config change once we're past the smoke test anyway
ok for a throwaway demo with fake data that's fine, just please put a giant TODO in the config so nobody ships it that way
already done, big scary comment block. swap target is the procured residency-bound endpoint before any pilot data
thank you. consumer API is a hard no the instant real or pilot data is in scope, but synthetic smoke test i'm not going to fight you on
got the demo notes flowing into the epic sandbox. they show up exactly the way the design doc described
remind me of the exact behavior there? want to make sure i'm describing it right to the clinical leads

per the integration design from 2025-10-02: generated scribe notes land in Epic as unsigned drafts that a clinician must review and sign off before they enter the patient record. nothing auto-commits, the human is always in the loop
perfect, that's the headline the clinicians care about. the AI never writes to the chart on its own
and that's exactly why this design passed my review. unsigned draft + mandatory clinician signoff is the control that makes the whole thing defensible
demo end to end works. fake transcript in, draft note out, lands in epic sandbox as an unsigned draft. will record a quick loom for the team
love it. next milestone is the compliant endpoint swap + BAA before we touch anything real. adding to the tracker
